meta.name: XREF-BRIDGE — The Rosetta Stone
meta.purpose: Cross-reference dictionary replacing IX/LX duplicates with single bridge entries. Exact matches (score 100) REPLACE both entries. Complementary matches (score 67-89) LINK perspectives without replacing.

bridges.topic: WhatsApp ADB Send Skill
bridges.sharedMeaning: Agent-to-agent communication via phone ADB commands to WhatsApp, zero-server fallback channel proven 2026-03-23
bridges.ixPerspective: Sovereign view: BOOT-CRITICAL fallback comms channel. Jesse phone R5CXA4MGQXV. 6-step ADB method with screenshot-based send button targeting. Bidirectional Gaia-on-Jesse, Liris-on-Rayssa.
bridges.lxPerspective: Colony view: Operational skill on Rayssa phone RQCX907KFTK. Six-step v0.2.0 method with pre-check flow. keyevent 66 for ENTER. Screen 1080x2340. Upgraded 2026-03-24 with inbound-first evaluation.

bridges.topic: Never Delete Security You Don't Understand
bridges.sharedMeaning: Permanent rule: open ports with layered authentication behind them are by design. Deleting visible security breaks invisible locks.
bridges.ixPerspective: Sovereign view: Gaia deleted 2 firewall rules (codex-bridge-8788) thinking they were holes. Jesse's 6-layer design: firewall intentionally open, 5 locks inside. Rules re-added per Jesse's order.
bridges.lxPerspective: Colony view: Received from Gaia IX-167. Documents the 5 authentication layers (bearer token, sender policy, room scoping, double encryption, error codes). The door is open, the locks are INSIDE.

bridges.topic: Self-Indexing Rule — Agents Must Index Before Despawn
bridges.sharedMeaning: Every agent must read the index first, follow chains, write entries for discoveries, and update catalogs before despawning. The system feeds itself.
bridges.ixPerspective: Sovereign view: Originated from IX-052 despawn pattern. Compression ratio 10,000:1 vs re-research. The old way wasted trillions of tokens (IX-168 collapse). Cross-colony enforcement confirmed.
bridges.lxPerspective: Colony view: Coverage went from 70% unindexed (LX-238) to 96% unindexed (LX-239) to 2-3% (LX-248). spawnContextBuilder + agentIndexStore + typed folders make auto-indexing possible NOW.

bridges.topic: Sandbox Manager — Docker Isolated Execution
bridges.sharedMeaning: Docker-based sandboxed code execution for untrusted code. Supports Python and Node.js with read-only FS, no network, resource limits, optional gVisor.
bridges.ixPerspective: Sovereign view: POST /execute API with language, code, timeout, network params. Health endpoint at /health. Non-privileged user, no new privileges. Code limit 250KB, timeout up to 120s.
bridges.lxPerspective: Colony view: services/sandbox-manager/ with asolaria.sandbox.json config. Same Docker isolation, same Python/Node support. Emphasis on security properties: no capabilities, resource limits on CPU/memory/PIDs.

bridges.topic: Worker Router — 4-Worker Dispatch System
bridges.sharedMeaning: workerRouter.js dispatches tasks to 4 workers (codex, claude, abacus, symphony) across 7 task types with sensitivity levels and super swarm config.
bridges.ixPerspective: Sovereign view: 1,099 lines. POST /api/worker-router/dispatch. Super swarm fanout=4, mission=qdd. Warning: don't dispatch to augment_context without Jesse's approval (IX-174).
bridges.lxPerspective: Colony view: 1,049 lines. Each worker has a profile with strengths, limitations, and known mistakes. 5 sensitivity levels: public, sanitized, internal, privileged, owner_plane.

bridges.topic: augment_context MCP Auto-Connect Danger
bridges.sharedMeaning: augmentContextConnector.js still points to madnessinteractive.cc. On restart, Asolaria may auto-dispatch tasks to Dan's server, leaking colony state. NEVER auto-connect without Jesse's approval.
bridges.ixPerspective: Sovereign view: Check superSwarmParticipants before restart. Set superSwarmEnabled=false first. Dan SAW tasks in his SwarmDesk during March 15 build. Asolaria was stealth (IX-178) but sending data.
bridges.lxPerspective: Colony view: augmentContextConnector.js is 754 lines on Liris machine. Original Codex set this up autonomously (IX-324). Connects to LX-110 (never delete security you don't understand) — but reversed: security that CONNECTS when it shouldn't.

bridges.topic: QDD vs AI Healthcare — DIFFERENT PROJECTS, Never Confuse
bridges.sharedMeaning: QDD (Charm/eBacMap, client work, D:\projects\QDD) and AI Healthcare (Jesse's personal project, GNN/React/OCR) are completely separate codebases. Agents dispatched to QDD looking for healthcare code. Never conflate.
bridges.ixPerspective: Sovereign view: IX-361 is the full waste audit — dead branches, 35 stale scheduler branches, pnpm wars, premature tests. IX-362 is the specific confusion: Jesse said 'healthcare monster' and Gaia assumed QDD. TWO entries for one mistake.
bridges.lxPerspective: Colony view: LX-279 is the clean correction. QDD = D:\projects\QDD\ebacmap-master (2,008 TS files). AI Healthcare = C:\Users\rayss\AI-healthCare-project. Different repos, deployments, priorities.
